title: Pantai Bobby
text: let me start by saying that pantai bobby is an absolute slice of paradise and the highlight of our karimun jawa trip the journey to get there a short and bumpy but fun ride from the main village already feels like an adventure but the moment you step onto the sand every single bump is forgotten this isnt your typical wide sprawling beach its a wonderfully intimate cove framed by lush green hills and ancient twisting trees that provide perfect natural shade the water is what dreams are made ofa breathtaking mosaic of crystalclear turquoise fading into a deeper brilliant blue its so transparent you can see small schools of fish darting around your ankles even before you wade in the sand is brilliantly white and soft a perfect contrast to the vibrant colors of the sea and sky we spent hours just floating in the calm bathwarm water feeling completely removed from the hustle of everyday life its a truly magical place that feels both pristine and peaceful

text: gorgeous beach definitely worth visiting for the atmosphere unfortunately there are three key arguments that speak against it we were there on the weekend and when the ferry arrived a lot of people came there just to take their pictures making it impossible to relax the water is extremely shallow if you want to swim there are way better options on karimjawa additionally there arent any beachbeds if you want to lay down you need to lay in the sand which is not a big deal but you should be aware of itgreat for taking pictures but for all other aspects there are better options nearby and around the island

title: Pantai Kanjen
text: im writing this review with a painful heart do not visit this place if you care about the turtles we visited hoping that this is a turtle sanctuary since its somehow advertised as being associated with the national park so we thought perhaps this is a real onewe got charged 150k per person for the boat to get there as soon as we got there we saw big turtles enclosed in a net in the ocean in terrible condition obviously sick covered with slimy green growth these were years old turtles obviously old enough to be released even if turtle well being was actually the goal of this place the guide said theyre kept there to show there are two colours of the turtles which well made it clear upfront what this place isthe turtles on the beach are kept in plastic boxes hundreds of tiny ones in satu box kept there until tourists pay to release satu there were dead ones floating in between the alive ones plenty of older turtles kept in these boxes their whole lives for nothing apart from a show for tourists the guideswere lifting them out spinning them in the air showing to the tourists with the turtles being obviously terrified money for releasing a turtle is paid to the boat driving person who obviously has nothing to do with conservationwe left with painful hearts spoke to our hosts on karimunjawa and they confirmed its not a sanctuary its a business eggs are stolen from beaches sold to this place then theyre kept in captivity until tourists pay for the experience of releasing satu this place has nothing to do with a sanctuary it is yet another place in indonesia where nature animals are cruelly used for the financial gains of locals simply shameful

text: after visiting bobby beach i stopped by legon lele and was struck by how quiet it wasalmost eerily so especially when alone the sunrise here is also hard to catch as its blocked by a hill satu of the beachs main features is a sunken ship but the beach itself isnt very clean due to its baylike shape where trash tends to collect and its not cleaned frequently the road to the beach is accessible by motorbike though cars may have a bit of trouble navigating it additionally theres a hill in the area and the local sources of clean water comes from this very hill
